description: Scale rblock geometry by a specified factor.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Use itasca.command('rblock scale ...') via command interface

keywords.name: diameter
keywords.description: Scale the rigid block to have volume of an equivalent {disk with unit thickness in 2D; sphere in 3D} with a specified diameter.

keywords.name: fish-size
keywords.description: Use the FISH function with name sfish to scale the “size” of the rigid block to fval . The FISH function must take a single argument that is a rigid block pointer and return a floating point value corresponding to a linear measure of the rigid block size.

keywords.name: keep-inertial
keywords.description: Scale the rblocks for contact detection but keep the volume and moment of inertia unchanged from before scaling.

keywords.name: relative
keywords.description: Scale the rigid block to have volume equal to frat multiplied by the current volume.

keywords.name: volume
keywords.description: Scale the rigid block to have volume fvol .
